english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Romans

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico ergo: Numquid Deus repulit populum suum? Absit. Nam et ego Israëlita sum ex semine Abraham, de tribu Benjamin:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω οὖν μὴ ἀπώσατο ὁ θεὸς τὸν λαὸν αὐτοῦ μὴ γένοιτο καὶ γὰρ ἐγὼ ἰσραηλίτης εἰμί ἐκ σπέρματος ἀβραάμ φυλῆς βενιαμίν
verses.king_james: I say then, Hath God cast away his people? God forbid. For I also am an Israelite, of the seed of Abraham, *of* the tribe of Benjamin.

verses.latin_vulgate: non repulit Deus plebem suam, quam præscivit. An nescitis in Elia quid dicit Scriptura? quemadmodum interpellat Deum adversum Israël:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἀπώσατο ὁ θεὸς τὸν λαὸν αὐτοῦ ὃν προέγνω ἢ οὐκ οἴδατε ἐν ἠλίᾳ τί λέγει ἡ γραφή ὡς ἐντυγχάνει τῷ θεῷ κατὰ τοῦ ἰσραήλ λέγων,
verses.king_james: God hath not cast away his people which he foreknew. Wot ye not what the scripture saith of Elias? how he maketh intercession to God against Israel, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, prophetas tuos occiderunt, altaria tua suffoderunt: et ego relictus sum solus, et quærunt animam meam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κύριε τοὺς προφήτας σου ἀπέκτειναν καὶ τὰ θυσιαστήριά σου κατέσκαψαν κἀγὼ ὑπελείφθην μόνος καὶ ζητοῦσιν τὴν ψυχήν μου
verses.king_james: Lord, they have killed thy prophets, and digged down thine altars; and I am left alone, and they seek my life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed quid dicit illi divinum responsum? Reliqui mihi septem millia virorum, qui non curvaverunt genua ante Baal.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ τί λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ χρηματισμός κατέλιπον ἐμαυτῷ ἑπτακισχιλίους ἄνδρας οἵτινες οὐκ ἔκαμψαν γόνυ τῇ βάαλ
verses.king_james: But what saith the answer of God unto him? I have reserved to myself seven thousand men, who have not bowed the knee to *the image of* Baal.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic ergo et in hoc tempore reliquiæ secundum electionem gratiæ salvæ factæ sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως οὖν καὶ ἐν τῷ νῦν καιρῷ λεῖμμα κατ᾽ ἐκλογὴν χάριτος γέγονεν
verses.king_james: Even so then at this present time also there is a remnant according to the election of grace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem gratia, jam non ex operibus: alioquin gratia jam non est gratia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ χάριτι οὐκέτι ἐξ ἔργων ἐπεὶ ἡ χάρις οὐκέτι γίνεται χάρις εἰ δὲ ἐξ ἔργων οὐκέτι ἐστὶν χάρις ἐπεὶ τὸ ἔργον οὐκέτι ἐστίν ἔργον
verses.king_james: And if by grace, then *is it* no more of works: otherwise grace is no more grace. But if *it be* of works, then is it no more grace: otherwise work is no more work.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid ergo? Quod quærebat Israël, hoc non est consecutus: electio autem consecuta est: ceteri vero excæcati sunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί οὖν ὃ ἐπιζητεῖ ἰσραήλ τούτου οὐκ ἐπέτυχεν ἡ δὲ ἐκλογὴ ἐπέτυχεν οἱ δὲ λοιποὶ ἐπωρώθησαν
verses.king_james: What then? Israel hath not obtained that which he seeketh for; but the election hath obtained it, and the rest were blinded

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut scriptum est: Dedit illis Deus spiritum compunctionis: oculos ut non videant, et aures ut non audiant, usque in hodiernum diem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς γέγραπται ἕδωκεν αὐτοῖς ὁ θεὸς πνεῦμα κατανύξεως ὀφθαλμοὺς τοῦ μὴ βλέπειν καὶ ὦτα τοῦ μὴ ἀκούειν ἕως τῆς σήμερον ἡμέρας
verses.king_james: (According as it is written, God hath given them the spirit of slumber, eyes that they should not see, and ears that they should not hear;) unto this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et David dicit: Fiat mensa eorum in laqueum, et in captionem, et in scandalum, et in retributionem illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ δαβὶδ λέγει γενηθήτω ἡ τράπεζα αὐτῶν εἰς παγίδα καὶ εἰς θήραν καὶ εἰς σκάνδαλον καὶ εἰς ἀνταπόδομα αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And David saith, Let their table be made a snare, and a trap, and a stumblingblock, and a recompence unto them:

verses.latin_vulgate: Obscurentur oculi eorum ne videant: et dorsum eorum semper incurva.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σκοτισθήτωσαν οἱ ὀφθαλμοὶ αὐτῶν τοῦ μὴ βλέπειν καὶ τὸν νῶτον αὐτῶν διαπαντός σύγκαμψον
verses.king_james: Let their eyes be darkened, that they may not see, and bow down their back alway.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico ergo: Numquid sic offenderunt ut caderent? Absit. Sed illorum delicto, salus est gentibus ut illos æmulentur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω οὖν μὴ ἔπταισαν ἵνα πέσωσιν μὴ γένοιτο ἀλλὰ τῷ αὐτῶν παραπτώματι ἡ σωτηρία τοῖς ἔθνεσιν εἰς τὸ παραζηλῶσαι αὐτούς
verses.king_james: I say then, Have they stumbled that they should fall? God forbid: but *rather* through their fall salvation *is come* unto the Gentiles, for to provoke them to jealousy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si delictum illorum divitiæ sunt mundi, et diminutio eorum divitiæ gentium: quanto magis plenitudo eorum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ τὸ παράπτωμα αὐτῶν πλοῦτος κόσμου καὶ τὸ ἥττημα αὐτῶν πλοῦτος ἐθνῶν πόσῳ μᾶλλον τὸ πλήρωμα αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Now if the fall of them *be* the riches of the world, and the diminishing of them the riches of the Gentiles; how much more their fulness?

verses.latin_vulgate: Vobis enim dico gentibus: Quamdiu quidem ego sum gentium Apostolus, ministerium meum honorificabo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμῖν γὰρ λέγω τοῖς ἔθνεσιν ἐφ᾽ ὅσον μὲν εἰμι ἐγὼ ἐθνῶν ἀπόστολος τὴν διακονίαν μου δοξάζω
verses.king_james: For I speak to you Gentiles, inasmuch as I am the apostle of the Gentiles, I magnify mine office:

verses.latin_vulgate: si quomodo ad æmulandum provocem carnem meam, et salvos faciam aliquos ex illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ πως παραζηλώσω μου τὴν σάρκα καὶ σώσω τινὰς ἐξ αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: If by any means I may provoke to emulation *them which are* my flesh, and might save some of them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim amissio eorum, reconciliatio est mundi: quæ assumptio, nisi vita ex mortuis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ ἡ ἀποβολὴ αὐτῶν καταλλαγὴ κόσμου τίς ἡ πρόσληψις εἰ μὴ ζωὴ ἐκ νεκρῶν
verses.king_james: For if the casting away of them *be* the reconciling of the world, what *shall* the receiving *of them be*, but life from the dead?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si delibatio sancta est, et massa: et si radix sancta, et rami.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ἡ ἀπαρχὴ ἁγία καὶ τὸ φύραμα καὶ εἰ ἡ ῥίζα ἁγία καὶ οἱ κλάδοι
verses.king_james: For if the firstfruit *be* holy, the lump *is* also *holy*: and if the root *be* holy, so *are* the branches.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si aliqui ex ramis fracti sunt, tu autem cum oleaster esses, insertus es in illis, et socius radicis, et pinguedinis olivæ factus es,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δέ τινες τῶν κλάδων ἐξεκλάσθησαν σὺ δὲ ἀγριέλαιος ὢν ἐνεκεντρίσθης ἐν αὐτοῖς καὶ συγκοινωνὸς τῆς ῥίζης καὶ τῆς πιότητος τῆς ἐλαίας ἐγένου
verses.king_james: And if some of the branches be broken off, and thou, being a wild olive tree, wert graffed in among them, and with them partakest of the root and fatness of the olive tree;

verses.latin_vulgate: noli gloriari adversus ramos. Quod si gloriaris: non tu radicem portas, sed radix te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ κατακαυχῶ τῶν κλάδων εἰ δὲ κατακαυχᾶσαι οὐ σὺ τὴν ῥίζαν βαστάζεις ἀλλ᾽ ἡ ῥίζα σέ
verses.king_james: Boast not against the branches. But if thou boast, thou bearest not the root, but the root thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dices ergo: Fracti sunt rami ut ego inserar.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐρεῖς οὖν ἐξεκλάσθησαν οἵ κλάδοι ἵνα ἐγὼ ἐγκεντρισθῶ
verses.king_james: Thou wilt say then, The branches were broken off, that I might be graffed in.

verses.latin_vulgate: Bene: propter incredulitatem fracti sunt. Tu autem fide stas: noli altum sapere, sed time.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καλῶς τῇ ἀπιστίᾳ ἐξεκλάσθησαν σὺ δὲ τῇ πίστει ἕστηκας μὴ ὑψηλοφρόνει, ἀλλὰ φοβοῦ
verses.king_james: Well; because of unbelief they were broken off, and thou standest by faith. Be not highminded, but fear:

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim Deus naturalibus ramis non pepercit: ne forte nec tibi parcat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ ὁ θεὸς τῶν κατὰ φύσιν κλάδων οὐκ ἐφείσατο μήπως οὐδὲ σοῦ φείσηται
verses.king_james: For if God spared not the natural branches, *take heed* lest he also spare not thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vide ergo bonitatem, et severitatem Dei: in eos quidem qui ceciderunt, severitatem: in te autem bonitatem Dei, si permanseris in bonitate, alioquin et tu excideris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἴδε οὖν χρηστότητα καὶ ἀποτομίαν θεοῦ ἐπὶ μὲν τοὺς πεσόντας ἀποτομίαν ἐπὶ δὲ σὲ χρηστότητα ἐὰν ἐπιμείνῃς τῇ χρηστότητι ἐπεὶ καὶ σὺ ἐκκοπήσῃ
verses.king_james: Behold therefore the goodness and severity of God: on them which fell, severity; but toward thee, goodness, if thou continue in *his* goodness: otherwise thou also shalt be cut off.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et illi, si non permanserint in incredulitate, inserentur: potens est enim Deus iterum inserere illos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκεῖνοι δέ ἐὰν μὴ ἐπιμείνωσιν τῇ ἀπιστίᾳ ἐγκεντρισθήσονται δυνατὸς γάρ ἐστιν ὁ θεὸς πάλιν ἐγκεντρίσαι αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And they also, if they abide not still in unbelief, shall be graffed in: for God is able to graff them in again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam si tu ex naturali excisus es oleastro, et contra naturam insertus es in bonam olivam: quanto magis ii qui secundum naturam inserentur suæ olivæ?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ σὺ ἐκ τῆς κατὰ φύσιν ἐξεκόπης ἀγριελαίου καὶ παρὰ φύσιν ἐνεκεντρίσθης εἰς καλλιέλαιον πόσῳ μᾶλλον οὗτοι οἱ κατὰ φύσιν ἐγκεντρισθήσονται τῇ ἰδίᾳ ἐλαίᾳ
verses.king_james: For if thou wert cut out of the olive tree which is wild by nature, and wert graffed contrary to nature into a good olive tree: how much more shall these, which be the natural *branches*, be graffed into their own olive tree?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolo enim vos ignorare, fratres, mysterium hoc (ut non sitis vobis ipsis sapientes), quia cæcitas ex parte contigit in Israël, donec plenitudo gentium intraret,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ θέλω ὑμᾶς ἀγνοεῖν ἀδελφοί τὸ μυστήριον τοῦτο ἵνα μὴ ἦτε παρ᾽ ἑαυτοῖς φρόνιμοι ὅτι πώρωσις ἀπὸ μέρους τῷ ἰσραὴλ γέγονεν ἄχρις οὗ τὸ πλήρωμα τῶν ἐθνῶν εἰσέλθῃ
verses.king_james: For I would not, brethren, that ye should be ignorant of this mystery, lest ye should be wise in your own conceits; that blindness in part is happened to Israel, until the fulness of the Gentiles be come in.

verses.latin_vulgate: et sic omnis Israël salvus fieret, sicut scriptum est: Veniet ex Sion, qui eripiat, et avertat impietatem a Jacob.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὕτως πᾶς ἰσραὴλ σωθήσεται καθὼς γέγραπται ἥξει ἐκ σιὼν ὁ ῥυόμενος καὶ ἀποστρέψει ἀσεβείας ἀπὸ ἰακώβ
verses.king_james: And so all Israel shall be saved: as it is written, There shall come out of Sion the Deliverer, and shall turn away ungodliness from Jacob:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hoc illis a me testamentum: cum abstulero peccata eorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὕτη αὐτοῖς ἡ παρ᾽ ἐμοῦ διαθήκη ὅταν ἀφέλωμαι τὰς ἁμαρτίας αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: For this *is* my covenant unto them, when I shall take away their sins.

verses.latin_vulgate: Secundum Evangelium quidem, inimici propter vos: secundum electionem autem, carissimi propter patres.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατὰ μὲν τὸ εὐαγγέλιον ἐχθροὶ δι᾽ ὑμᾶς κατὰ δὲ τὴν ἐκλογὴν ἀγαπητοὶ διὰ τοὺς πατέρας
verses.king_james: As concerning the gospel, *they are* enemies for your sakes: but as touching the election, *they are* beloved for the fathers' sakes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sine pœnitentia enim sunt dona et vocatio Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμεταμέλητα γὰρ τὰ χαρίσματα καὶ ἡ κλῆσις τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: For the gifts and calling of God *are* without repentance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut enim aliquando et vos non credidistis Deo, nunc autem misericordiam consecuti estis propter incredulitatem illorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὥσπερ γὰρ καὶ ὑμεῖς ποτε ἠπειθήσατε τῷ θεῷ νῦν δὲ ἠλεήθητε τῇ τούτων ἀπειθείᾳ
verses.king_james: For as ye in times past have not believed God, yet have now obtained mercy through their unbelief:

verses.latin_vulgate: ita et isti nunc non crediderunt in vestram misericordiam: ut et ipsi misericordiam consequantur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως καὶ οὗτοι νῦν ἠπείθησαν τῷ ὑμετέρῳ ἐλέει ἵνα καὶ αὐτοὶ ἐλεηθῶσιν
verses.king_james: Even so have these also now not believed, that through your mercy they also may obtain mercy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conclusit enim Deus omnia in incredulitate, ut omnium misereatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνέκλεισεν γὰρ ὁ θεὸς τοὺς πάντας εἰς ἀπείθειαν ἵνα τοὺς πάντας ἐλεήσῃ
verses.king_james: For God hath concluded them all in unbelief, that he might have mercy upon all.

verses.latin_vulgate: O altitudo divitiarum sapientiæ, et scientiæ Dei: quam incomprehensibilia sunt judicia ejus, et investigabiles viæ ejus!
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὦ βάθος πλούτου καὶ σοφίας καὶ γνώσεως θεοῦ ὡς ἀνεξερεύνητα τὰ κρίματα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀνεξιχνίαστοι αἱ ὁδοὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: O the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of God! how unsearchable *are* his judgments, and his ways past finding out!

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis enim cognovit sensum Domini? aut quis consiliarius ejus fuit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίς γὰρ ἔγνω νοῦν κυρίου ἢ τίς σύμβουλος αὐτοῦ ἐγένετο
verses.king_james: For who hath known the mind of the Lord? or who hath been his counsellor?

verses.latin_vulgate: aut quis prior dedit illi, et retribuetur ei?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ τίς προέδωκεν αὐτῷ καὶ ἀνταποδοθήσεται αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: Or who hath first given to him, and it shall be recompensed unto him again?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam ex ipso, et per ipsum, et in ipso sunt omnia: ipsi gloria in sæcula. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἐξ αὐτοῦ καὶ δι᾽ αὐτοῦ καὶ εἰς αὐτὸν τὰ πάντα αὐτῷ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας ἀμήν
verses.king_james: For of him, and through him, and to him, *are* all things: to whom *be* glory for ever. Amen.
